title: Epic: mvp-sync — distributed edit + merge driver
body:
Goal: two clones can both edit bugs while offline, `jjf push` /
`jjf pull` round-trip the `issues` bookmark via standard git
transport, and `jjf sync` resolves any conflicts automatically
without human intervention.

# Approach

The big surprise from research (`8d3e045`, archived): jj's automatic
content merge isn't enough for bug data. The moment two clones touch
nearby lines in the same bug file (title vs status, status vs a new
comment), jj's content merge produces conflicts. The natural
single-line-per-key JSON layout puts fields one line apart, well
inside the merger's "too close" threshold.

The original fix was a **file-bytes merge driver** that parsed jj's
conflict markers and ran a per-field policy. Later (in
`sync-conflict-fallback`) this was superseded by an **op-space
resolver** in `crates/jjf-storage/src/merge_ops.rs` — the file is a
deterministic projection of the op chain, divergence resolves in
op-space, conflicts cannot surface.

Per-field policy (final, see `docs/storage-format.md` §6):

- **Scalars** (status, title, assignee, body): last-write-wins by
  `Jjf-At:` trailer timestamp.
- **Sets** (labels, dependencies): causal — each add/remove
  tracked per key; final state is `present` iff the last write for
  that key was an add.
- **Comments**: union by `comment_id`; comments are append-only.

`jjf push` / `pull` are wrappers over `jj git push --bookmark issues` /
`jj git fetch`, plus the op-space resolver hook on fetch divergence.

# Child tickets

## Pre-cutover (git-bug era, all shipped)

- `e2e473b` — file-bytes merge driver (legacy, library stays in workspace).
- `07780aa` — `sync-remote-setup`: `jjf remote add|ls|rm` wraps `jj git remote`.
- `ed7f46b` — `sync-push-pull`: push/pull verbs end-to-end.
- `bfc732b` — `sync-conflict-fallback`: op-space resolver replaces file-bytes path.

## Post-cutover quality sweep (2026-06-22, all shipped)

- `1c20aa3` — `sync-resolve-divergence-tests`: 34 unit tests covering the
  per-field merge policy directly. `reduce_to_merged` extracted as a pure
  folder so tests need no `JjRepo`. Pinned the cross-version
  unstamped-before-stamped ordering invariant.
- `004dd23` — `sync-investigate-dual-file-filter`: verdict Path B. The
  four-path filter in `history.rs` IS load-bearing — same-second comment
  appends with byte-identical JSON drop out without the dual-file entry.
  Regression test pins the empirical case.
- `0896e4f` — `sync-reenable-merge-cross-check`: dropped the
  `touched_by_merge` guard. The cross-check surfaced a real divergence:
  `replay_ops` walked jj-log order while the resolver wrote in spec §6 LWW
  order. Refactored `replay_ops` to share the resolver's `OpKey` via
  `sort_entries_lww`; cross-check now runs unconditionally and agrees by
  construction.

# Depends on

- `mvp-storage` (defines the on-disk file format the merge driver works against).

# Blocks

- `multi-client` (the daemon model assumes cross-machine sync works).

# Status

**Done** as of 2026-06-22. Push/pull/merge loop works end-to-end with
op-space resolution; cross-check runs unconditionally and agrees with
the resolver by construction; per-field policy directly unit-tested
(34 tests); same-second comment-walk and LWW-replay-vs-resolver
regressions pinned. 267 workspace tests green.

Epic re-filed in jjforge 2026-06-22 during the git-bug cutover
(decision in archived `d12031c`). Full pre-cutover history with
subagent findings lives in archived git-bug — use `git-bug bug show
436ac0a` against `refs/bugs/*`.
